name: pit-selector-changed
notes: Operational pitfall: a selector-bearing run must still be recorded and compared like any other invocation -- two runs with the identical selector (`tests/alpha.test.ts`) are exact-comparable and previous-comparable correctly resolves the earlier one as baseline. But when the selector changes between runs (`tests/alpha.test.ts` -> `tests/beta.test.ts`), the MVP adapter declares no `selector-relation` capability, so containment between the two scopes is unproven: the comparison must abstain (`comparability: 'none'`, reason `selector-relation-unknown`) with a `selector-changed` surface event rather than silently comparing across incompatible scopes or guessing a verdict. A marker commit between A and B keeps the two same-selector runs content-distinct (different tree) so the store does not collapse them to a single idempotent run_id (§3.5/§5.8).

steps.message: advance tree before B (keep A and B content-distinct)
